description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex` (module `MutagenEx.CoverageRunner`)
  implementing [mutagen.coverage](.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md) r1-r5, r7.
  Aborts cleanly if cover already running. Forces `max_cases: 1` and
  configured `seed`. Stops cover idempotently in `try/rescue`.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex` (module `MutagenEx.Baseline`) implementing
  [mutagen.mutation_pipeline](.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md) r1, r2.
  Aborts pipeline on red baseline; emits the failures list.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex` (module `MutagenEx.AstCache`) implementing
  [mutagen.coverage](.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md) r6. Single-load,
  immutable, keeps verbatim source text alongside the AST.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex` (module `MutagenEx.MutationRunner`)
  implementing [mutagen.mutation_pipeline](.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md) r3-r11.
  Refuses self-mutation; classifies into 5 outcomes; restores via
  `Code.compile_quoted/1` on cached AST; emits state-drift warning for
  `use SomeModule` modules.
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex` (private module
  `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.MutationLoop`) per [mutagen.decision.mutation_loop_private](.spec/decisions/mutation_loop_private.md).
  Owns per-mutation timeout via `Task.async + Task.yield + Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill)`,
  stdout/stderr suppression, and state snapshotting.
- Integration tests at `test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs`,
  `.../baseline_test.exs`, `.../ast_cache_test.exs`, `.../mutation_runner_test.exs`.
  All use `on_exit/1` for `:cover.stop` defensive cleanup (per H-Tt3).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test --include integration test/mutagen_ex/`.
  Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.coverage.v1`-`.v3`,
  `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1`, `.v2`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.coverage.s1` through `s7`,
  scenarios `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.s1` through `s8`.
- The C1 and C2 spike tests from S2 must still pass; they are regression
  for the primary state-hygiene invariants this stage implements.
- The implementer must run S5's test suite at least 3 consecutive times
  locally before declaring merge-gate-green; any flake = state-hygiene bug.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex` (S6)
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` orchestration body (S6 wires it; this stage
  only fills out the dispatch-table callees)
- `test/fixtures/lane_project/**` (S7)
- Anything under `test/mutagen_ex/golden/`

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not promote `MutationLoop` to a peer module. It is private to the
  runner per [mutagen.decision.mutation_loop_private](.spec/decisions/mutation_loop_private.md).
- Do not add per-mutation linked-process tracking via `Process.monitor/1`.
  v1 accepts the coarse `Process.registered()` signal — finer tracking is
  gold-plating.
- Do not introduce a "3 consecutive timeouts → hard abort" branch.
  Per [mutagen.decision.timeout_handling](.spec/decisions/timeout_handling.md),
  each timeout is `:timeout` in `mutation.results` and the runner continues.
- Do not introduce a `--strict-restore` flag. The state-drift warning in
  the JSON is the only signal v1 ships.
- Do not retry red-baseline tests. The judge can re-invoke the tool.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] Integration test suite passes 3× consecutively
- [ ] S2 spike tests still pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.coverage.v1`-`.v3`, `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1`, `.v2`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: agent-a081615917930f47d (worker subagent under orchestrator session fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9/subagents/agent-a081615917930f47d.jsonl

Commit: a4b2c737103ee4159ce5651ff25a72d0a2187c88
Branch: mutagen-wrd.7/coverage-baseline-runner-loop (worktree /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-mutagen-wrd.7, branched off epic/mutagen-wrd @ 1c4757d)

Files touched (9 new + 2 spec stub flips, all per Allowed touches + the orchestrator-authorized stub-flip expansion):
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (new) — implements mutagen.coverage r6
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex (new) — implements mutagen.mutation_pipeline r1, r2
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (new) — implements mutagen.coverage r1-r5, r7
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (new) — implements mutagen.mutation_pipeline r3-r11
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (new; @moduledoc false) — private helper per mutagen.decision.mutation_loop_private
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs (new; 10 tests)
- test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs (new; 8 tests)
- test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs (new; 12 tests)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs (new; 16 tests)
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md — verification stubs v1/v2/v3/v4 flipped execute: false → true
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md — verification stubs v1/v2/v3/v4 flipped execute: false → true

Tests added: 46 total (MutagenEx.AstCacheTest, MutagenEx.BaselineTest, MutagenEx.CoverageRunnerTest, MutagenEx.MutationRunnerTest). Every requirement r1-r11 of mutation_pipeline and r1-r7 of coverage has at least one named test asserting a concrete failure mode the requirement would produce. Scenarios s1-s7 (coverage) and s1-s8 (mutation_pipeline) each have a covering assertion.

Verification stubs flipped (orchestrator-authorized scope expansion per ticket prompt — "If flipping verification stubs in the relevant specs (coverage.spec.md / mutation_pipeline.spec.md / json_schema.spec.md per the ticket) from `execute: false` → `execute: true` is required by the merge gate, do it. Precedent: stages .1, .3, .4, .5, .6 all flipped stubs and got APPROVED."):
- mutagen.coverage.v1 (test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs)
- mutagen.coverage.v2 (--only scope_filter)
- mutagen.coverage.v3 (test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs)
- mutagen.coverage.v4 (test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs — pre-existing from S2; same merge-gate item)
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1 (test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs)
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v2 (test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs)
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v3 (test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs — pre-existing from S2)
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v4 (test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs — pre-existing from S2)

I did NOT flip mutagen.json_schema stubs — those reference S6's json_reporter tests which don't exist yet.

Audit-expanded scope: N/A (iteration 1). The .spec/specs/{coverage,mutation_pipeline}.spec.md flips are NEW scope-expansion claimed under the orchestrator's pre-authorization in the ticket prompt; documented clearly here so the verifier suppresses the mechanical scope flag for those two files. All other changed files match the ticket's Allowed touches globs literally.

Deferred must requirements: NONE — every Deliverable / Verification / Out-of-Scope (intent) item in the ticket is honored.

Out-of-Scope (intent) honored:
- MutationLoop is private (@moduledoc false; under lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/). Tests reach it through MutationRunner's surface.
- No Process.monitor/1 — taint detection uses Process.registered/0 + :ets.all/0 + :persistent_term.info/0 only.
- No "3 consecutive timeouts → hard abort" branch. Each timeout classifies :timeout and the runner continues.
- No --strict-restore flag. State-drift warnings are the only ship signal.
- No red-baseline retry. Baseline aborts on first red.

Project verification:
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean
- `mix test`: 1 doctest, 263 tests, 0 failures (a 46-test increase from epic HEAD's 217)
- 3× consecutive full-suite runs: 0 failures, no flakes
- C1 + C2 spike from S2 still pass (1 test each, ~19s for C1)
- Per-spec-stub commands all green:
  * mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs → 12 tests, 0 failures
  * mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs --only scope_filter → 2 tests, 0 failures
  * mix test test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs → 10 tests
  * mix test test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs → 8 tests
  * mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs → 16 tests
- mix format: my 9 files are formatted; pre-existing format drift in other files is OOS.

S2 spike generalization notes (per the orchestrator's escalation directive — "if S2's spike findings turn out to not generalize to the production code — that's a major signal, escalate via WORKER_ERROR"). They generalized cleanly:
1. :cover lives in OTP's `lib/tools-*/ebin` — production CoverageRunner replicates the path-append-and-Code.ensure_loaded shape (locate_tools_ebin/0 + ensure_cover_loadable/1).
2. :cover.compile_beam/1 needs the Dbgi chunk — `mix compile` for the :test env defaults to debug_info: true, so the project's .beam files already carry it. No compiler-option toggle needed in CoverageRunner.
3. ExUnit re-running shape (`%{async?: false, group: nil, parameterize: nil}`) — codified in MutationLoop's add_module call. If ExUnit's internal config shape changes in a future Elixir, this is the single line the S2 spike would catch as a regression.
4. :vsn attribute exclusion — only relevant for C1's attribute-delta check (a test concern); production code doesn't compare module attributes.

Summary: S5 brings the four production modules + private helper online with state-hygiene contracts pulled directly from S2's gated spike. CoverageRunner refuses cover_already_running, forces max_cases:1 + seed, stops cover idempotently, filters covered_lines to in-scope files. Baseline aborts on red and surfaces async:true module warnings. MutationRunner refuses self-mutation, classifies into exactly 5 outcomes, restores via Code.compile_quoted on cached AST, aborts on unrecoverable restore failure, emits state_drift_warning for `use SomeModule` scoped modules, propagates tainted_predecessors after any growth in process/ETS/persistent_term counts or any :timeout site. MutationLoop owns Task.async/yield/shutdown(:brutal_kill) per-mutation timeout, stderr capture, and pre/post state snapshots. AstCache is single-load with verbatim source preserved for downstream before_source slicing.

Worker exiting after IMPLEMENTED per --impl-only. Orchestrator owns verify, audit, and merge-back into epic/mutagen-wrd.

title: S5: Coverage + Baseline + AstCache + MutationRunner + MutationLoop
